configurations.name: with_skill
configurations.description: Using the human-voice-writer skill (iteration 3, stability re-benchmark)

evals.prompt: Write a 3-paragraph blog post about why remote work is here to stay. Target audience is HR professionals.

expectations.text: Output contains none of the Tier 1 banned AI vocabulary
Output contains no em dashes, en dashes, or double hyphens
Sentence length standard deviation is 7 or more words
Output has exactly 3 paragraphs as requested
Output does not open with a banned opening

expectations.evidence: Title uses negation-correction pair: 'Remote Work Isn't a Perk Anymore. It's the Baseline.'

expectations.text: Output contains none of the Tier 1 banned AI vocabulary
Output contains no em dashes, en dashes, or double hyphens
Sentence length standard deviation is 7 or more words
Output has exactly 3 paragraphs as requested
Output does not open with a banned opening
No em dashes, en dashes, or double hyphens
No em dashes, en dashes, or double hyphens

evals.prompt: Write a Slack message about database migration completion

expectations.text: No em dashes, en dashes, or double hyphens
No em dashes, en dashes, or double hyphens

comparison_with_iteration_2.change: with_skill dropped from 100% to 95% (1 assertion failure: negation-assertion pattern in blog title). without_skill dropped from 70% to 60% due to additional failures in burstiness and word count.

analyst_notes: with_skill passed 19/20 assertions (95%), down from 20/20 (100%) in iteration 2

The single with_skill failure was a negation-assertion pattern in the blog post title: 'Remote Work Isn't a Perk Anymore. It's the Baseline.' — the grader correctly flagged this as a negation-correction pair, though it appeared in a heading rather than body text
This suggests the skill's self-review pass doesn't reliably catch negation-assertion patterns in titles/headings
without_skill baseline dropped to 60% from 70% in iteration 2 — the blog post performed worse this run (43% vs 71%) with new failures in burstiness (SD=4.7) and paragraph count (4 instead of 3)
Em dashes remain the most consistent baseline failure: without_skill used em dashes in all 3 evals (blog, de-ai, slack)
Slack with_skill output was excellent: 16 words, natural tone, single contraction — a strong demonstration of length calibration
Slack without_skill was 58 words with emoji and multiple paragraphs — classic AI over-writing for format
Token overhead is much lower this iteration (+7% vs +32% in iteration 2) because the skill was provided inline to agents rather than read from disk
Duration overhead similarly lower (+59% vs +188% in iteration 2) for the same reason
De-AI marketing with_skill achieved perfect 7/7, successfully removing all AI patterns including the false-choice framing that without_skill retained
